The strongest positive factor is the complete absence of frost risk and suitable mean temperature. However, severe drought stress will be the primary limiting factor. Operational risks include widespread crop failure, extremely low yields, and poor nutrient uptake without significant water input. Mitigation requires mandatory supplemental irrigation, selection of highly drought-tolerant varieties, and implementation of water-efficient farming practices. Alternative, more drought-adapted crops should be considered.
